backend/main.mo: Main backend actor implementing video clip management, user profiles with role-based access control, YouTube OAuth integration, clip similarity scoring, trending analytics, and admin functions for managing user roles and permissions.
backend/migration.mo: Migration module that transforms old user profiles without roles to new profiles with a default 'user' role, preserving existing YouTube and Google OAuth credentials.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting two implemented features: admin panel UI for user status management and user role badge display on the home screen, both with high confidence.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Comprehensive summaries of all frontend components, hooks, and configuration files including OAuth integration, clip management, admin panel, role-based UI elements, and styling configuration.
frontend/.env.example: Environment variable template with detailed step-by-step instructions for obtaining and configuring Google OAuth Client ID, including troubleshooting guidance for common OAuth issues.
frontend/.gitignore: Git ignore configuration excluding node_modules, build artifacts, editor files, logs, and environment variable files from version control.
frontend/index.css: Global Tailwind CSS styles with custom OKLCH color system defining design tokens for light and dark modes, including primary, secondary, accent, and semantic colors.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the React application with title 'Beast Clipping' and root div for mounting the app.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Main React application component with routing, authentication flow, profile setup, video URL management, clip creation interface, AI suggestions state, and admin panel integration using TanStack Router and React Query.
Displays detailed authentication debug information for admins, including principal IDs, admin status, hash map size, and character-by-character string comparison for troubleshooting. Provides refresh functionality and clipboard copy for debugging values.
frontend/src/components/AdminErrorBounda: React error boundary component that catches and displays errors in the admin panel with detailed error messages, stack traces, and component stacks. Provides retry and home navigation options.
frontend/src/components/AdminList.tsx: Displays a list of current admin users with their principal IDs and generated short user IDs. Shows loading states, error handling, and empty states when no admins exist.
Form component for adding new admins by entering their full Principal ID. Includes validation, success/error messaging, and integrates the AdminList component to display current admins.
frontend/src/components/AdminPanel.tsx: Main admin dashboard that aggregates admin statistics, trending analytics, and collapsible sections for debug panel, admin management, and user status management. Includes loading and error states with retry functionality.
frontend/src/components/ChannelConnectio: Manages YouTube channel connection via Google OAuth, displaying connection status with channel avatar and name. Provides connect/disconnect functionality with loading states and configuration checks.
frontend/src/components/ClipCard.tsx: Displays a single video clip card with thumbnail, title, viral score, duration, and action buttons for deleting, posting to YouTube, and downloading. Includes YouTube channel connection status checks and confirmation dialogs.
frontend/src/components/ClipList.tsx: Renders a scrollable list of saved video clips using ClipCard components, with loading states and empty state messaging. Handles clip selection and deletion.
Displays either AI-generated clip suggestions or trending viral clips with viral scores, timestamps, and action buttons to use suggested clips. Switches between AI and trending modes based on available data.
frontend/src/components/ClipTimestampCon: Provides a form for creating clips with title input and start/end timestamp controls (minutes/seconds). Handles clip creation, validation, and syncs with selected clips or AI suggestions.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Main application layout component with header, navigation, authentication controls (Internet Identity login/logout), and conditional admin panel access. Includes user role display and debug logging for admin visibility.
frontend/src/components/ProfileSetup.tsx: First-time user profile setup form collecting name and email with validation. Saves user profile with default 'user' role and triggers completion callback.
frontend/src/components/UserIdDisplay.ts: Displays the current user's short ID and full principal ID with copy-to-clipboard functionality for both values.
frontend/src/components/UserRoleBadge.ts: Renders a styled badge component showing user roles (Owner, Admin, User, Friend) with corresponding icons and color schemes.
frontend/src/components/UserStatusManage: Admin interface for managing user roles with search functionality, displaying a table of users with their principals and allowing role changes via dropdown.
frontend/src/components/VideoUrlForm.tsx: Form component for entering YouTube URLs with validation, video loading, and AI clip generation functionality.
Displays a viral score badge with color-coded styling and icons based on score thresholds (high/medium/low viral potential).
frontend/src/components/YouTubePlayer.ts: Embeds a YouTube video player using an iframe with responsive 16:9 aspect ratio.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Manages the backend actor instance with authentication, creating authenticated or anonymous actors based on Internet Identity state and invalidating dependent queries on actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useAddAdmin.ts: Mutation hook for adding admin users by user ID, invalidating admin-related queries on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminDebug.ts: Query hook for fetching admin debug information including current principal, admin status, and admin list (backend method not yet fully implemented).
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminList.ts: Query hook that fetches the list of admin principals from the backend.
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminStats.ts: Query hook that fetches admin statistics including total clips count and trending clips analytics.
Provides a mutation hook to auto-generate clip suggestions for a video, currently using mock AI data with viral scores while the backend method is being developed.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipCreation.ts: Mutation hook for creating video clips that calculates a viral score based on duration and title characteristics, then saves the clip via the backend actor.
frontend/src/hooks/useClips.ts: Query hook to fetch all clips and provides a mutation to delete clips, tracking deletion state and invalidating queries on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipTimestamps.ts: Manages clip timestamp state (start/end minutes/seconds), provides validation, formatting, and conversion utilities for time ranges.
frontend/src/hooks/useDownloadClip.ts: Mutation hook that generates a download URL from the backend and triggers a browser download for a video clip segment with a sanitized filename.
frontend/src/hooks/useGetOwnRole.ts: Query hook that fetches the current user's role from the backend, enabled only when the actor and identity are available.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides React context and hooks for Internet Identity authentication, managing login state, identity persistence, and AuthClient lifecycle with configurable options.
frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts: Query hook that checks if the current user is an admin by calling the backend, with extensive logging for debugging and proper dependency handling on actor and identity availability.
frontend/src/hooks/usePostToYouTube.ts: Mutation hook to post clips to YouTube as Shorts, validating OAuth connection and 60-second duration limit before calling the backend.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Collection of query and mutation hooks for user profile management, Google OAuth credential storage, and YouTube connection status checking.
frontend/src/hooks/useSetUserRole.ts: React hook that provides a mutation to set user roles via the backend actor, invalidating related queries and showing toast notifications on success or error.
frontend/src/hooks/useTrendingClips.ts: React hook that fetches trending video clips from the backend actor using React Query.
frontend/src/hooks/useUserRoles.ts: React hook that queries all user roles from the backend actor, returning an array of Principal-UserRole tuples.
frontend/src/hooks/useYouTubeChannel.ts: React hook managing YouTube channel connection status and OAuth flow, including mutations for connecting (via Google OAuth redirect) and disconnecting YouTube channels.
Utility hook providing functions to validate YouTube URLs and extract video IDs from YouTube links.
frontend/src/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom CSS variables for light and dark theme colors including primary, secondary, accent, and chart colors.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that sets up React Query, Internet Identity provider, and renders the root App component.
frontend/src/pages/OAuthCallback.tsx: OAuth callback page that processes Google authorization codes, stores credentials via backend actor, and displays success/error states before redirecting to home.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for extracting, storing, and managing URL parameters from both regular query strings and hash-based routing, with sessionStorage persistence support.
frontend/src/utils/userIdGenerator.ts: Generates deterministic 8-character user IDs from principal strings using a simple hash function.
frontend/src/utils/youtubeOAuthDebug.ts: Debugging utility class for tracking and logging YouTube OAuth flow events, validating OAuth responses, and identifying common error patterns.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration file that defines custom theme extensions including OKLCH color system with CSS variables, container settings, custom animations for accordions, and plugins for typography, container queries, and animations.
project_state.json: Project state tracking file for "Beast Clipping" that records existing features for user status display and management UI, plus pending feature requests to implement a backend user role system (Owner, Admin, User, Friend) with validation and query methods.
